revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:掃部寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:掃部寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:掃部寮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:掃部寮]]
'''Kamonryō''' (掃部寮) or '''Kamonzukasa''' was an [[extralegal office]] (令外官) under the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} (宮内省) in Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]]. In the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, it is referred to as "Kanemori no Tsukasa" (かにもりのつかさ). Its [[Tang name]] was "Shūkyūsho" (守宮署).
== History and Duties == The Kamonryō was responsible for setting up arrangements for palace ceremonies and cleaning the palace interiors. Its duties overlapped with those of the {{ill|Shudenryō|ja|主殿寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shudenryō|qq=}}, which managed the maintenance and upkeep of palace facilities. Members of the ''Kamon'' (掃部) group supported cleaning and arrangements, along with 80 workers known as ''kushichō'' (駆使丁), assigned for practical tasks.
The Kamonryō was established in {{ill|820|en|820|qid=Q24872|lt=820|1=820}} (11th year of the [[Kōnin era]]) as part of administrative reforms by merging the {{ill|Sōbumon|ja|掃部司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōbumon|1=Q67172918|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q67172918}} of the {{ill|Ōkurashō (Ritsuryō)|ja|大蔵省|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkurashō (Ritsuryō)|1=Ministry of the Treasury|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q910073}} with the {{ill|Naikamonmon|ja|内掃部司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikamonmon|1=Q67172955|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q67172955}} of the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Ministry of the Imperial Household|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}.
In later periods, the [[Nakahara clan]] assumed leadership of the Kamonryō, shifting its primary role to overseeing attire within the palace.
== Personnel == * '''Head (頭)''' - Equivalent to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (従五位下). Tang names: ''Shūkyūryō'' (守宮令), ''Saisōkyō'' (洒掃卿), ''Shōsha Hōgo'' (尚舎奉御). * '''Assistant (助)''' - Equivalent to [[:en:Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] (従六位上). Tang names: ''Saisō Shōin'' (洒掃少尹), ''Shōsha Chōchō'' (尚舎直長), ''Kansha Shōkan'' (官舎少監). * '''Administrator (允)''' - Equivalent to [[Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade]] (従七位上). Tang names: ''Shūkyūjō'' (守宮丞), ''Saisōin'' (洒掃尹). * '''Clerks (属)''': ** '''Daisoku''' (大属): Equivalent to [[Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade]] (従八位下). ** '''Shōsoku''' (少属): Equivalent to [[Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade]] (大初位上). Tang names: ''Shūkyūshi'' (守宮史), ''Saisōshi'' (洒掃史), ''Shōsha Reishi'' (尚舎令史). * '''Scribes (史生)'''. * '''Supervisors (寮掌)'''. * '''Attendants (使部)'''. * '''Assistants (直丁)'''. * '''Workers (駆使丁)'''. * '''Sweepers (掃部)'''.
== Origin of the Name "Kamon" == According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}, during the birth of {{ill|Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugaya Fukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373935}}, the ancestor of the ''Sōmori no Muraji'' (掃守連), {{ill|Ame no Oshihito no Mikoto|ja|天忍人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Oshihito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, constructed a birthing hut on the beach, made brooms, and swept away crabs to ensure the safety of the birthing platform. This act established their hereditary role in cleaning and arrangements, leading to the name "Kanemori" (蟹守, crab keepers).
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the ''Sōmori no Muraji'' lineage is traced to {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|海神豊玉彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s son, Furutama no Mikoto (振魂尊), through his fourth descendant, Ame no Oshihito no Mikoto, who served under Emperor [[Yūryaku]] as a supervisor of cleaning, receiving the surname ''Sōmori no Muraji''.
Historian {{ill|Eichi Ishigami|ja|石上英一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eichi Ishigami|1=Eiichi Ishigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584773}} suggests that the ''Sōmori no Muraji'' were hereditary cleaners and arrangers in the palace (伴造). Originally called "Kamimori" (殿上守, keepers of cleanliness), the name evolved into "Kanemori," and a retrospective etymology linking it to crabs ("Kani") was later fabricated.
== References == {{reflist}}
